issue.title: Perf: reduce terminal typing/output latency — singleton terminal-output listener + reader sleep floor

successCriteria.description: A single process-wide terminal-output/terminal-exit listener dispatches to all terminals by id; no per-terminal listen() calls remain in useTerminal
No dropped, reordered, or duplicated terminal bytes (dispatch is by exact id; single in-order listener)
Idle CPU does not regress — the reader does not busy-spin (idle sleep stays modest; short sleep only while bytes are pending)
successCriteria.note: design avoids busy-spin (idle 8ms); idle-CPU confirmation pending tauri:dev

successCriteria.description: pnpm build + pnpm -C apps/desktop test + cargo test --lib clean
Typing and heavy output feel snappier in tauri:dev (user-confirmed)

phases.title: Singleton terminal-output/exit listener with id dispatch map
phases.content:
In apps/desktop/src/components/terminal/useTerminal.ts, replace the PER-INSTANCE `listen("terminal-output", …)` / `listen("terminal-exit", …)` (currently registered in createTerminal, lines ~64-81) with a SINGLE process-wide listener pair plus a `Map<id, callbacks>` dispatch table at MODULE scope.

Design:
- Module scope: `type TerminalCallbacks = { onOutput?: (d: string) => void; onExit?: (c: number | null) => void }`; `const terminalCallbacks = new Map<string, TerminalCallbacks>()`; lazy init guard `let globalListeners: Promise<void> | null = null`.
- `async function ensureGlobalTerminalListeners()`: if `globalListeners` is set, return it; else assign a promise that does `listen<TerminalOutputEvent>("terminal-output", (e) => terminalCallbacks.get(e.payload.id)?.onOutput?.(e.payload.data))` and the same for `terminal-exit` → `?.onExit?.(e.payload.exitCode)`. Keep the unlisten fns module-scoped; the global listeners live for the app lifetime (do NOT unlisten per terminal).
- In `createTerminal`: `await ensureGlobalTerminalListeners()` BEFORE `invoke("create_terminal")`, then `terminalCallbacks.set(id, { onOutput: (d) => onOutputRef.current?.(d), onExit: (c) => { setIsConnected(false); onExitRef.current?.(c); } })`. (The wrapper reads the refs so callbacks stay live without re-registering.)
- In `destroyTerminal` and the unmount cleanup effect: replace the per-instance unlisten calls with `terminalCallbacks.delete(currentIdRef.current)` (guard for null). Remove `unlistenOutputRef`/`unlistenExitRef` (no longer needed) or leave them unused-and-removed.
- Preserve all existing behavior: id filtering (now via map key), isConnected on exit, error handling.

This cuts N×M listener wake-ups (every output event waking all N terminals' listeners) to 1×M. Dispatch is by exact id and a single listener processes events in order, so no byte drop/reorder.
verification: no per-terminal listen() calls remain in useTerminal.ts; a single module-level listener pair dispatches via terminalCallbacks Map
createTerminal registers id->callbacks; destroy/unmount deletes the map entry
tsc -b clean; pnpm -C apps/desktop test clean
phases.summary: useTerminal.ts: replaced per-terminal listen(terminal-output/exit) with a single module-level listener pair + terminalCallbacks Map<id,{onOutput,onExit}>. ensureGlobalTerminalListeners() lazily registers once (app-lifetime); createTerminal sets map entry (ref-reading wrappers), destroy/unmount/error delete it. Removed UnlistenFn import + unlisten refs. N×M wake-ups -> 1×M. Verified at combined gate.

phases.title: Reduce PTY reader latency floor (adaptive sleep + shorter flush) + final gate
phases.content:
In apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/terminal/pty.rs reader loop (around lines 244-352): the echo-latency floor is FLUSH_INTERVAL (10ms) plus the 10ms WouldBlock sleep. Reduce both, carefully, WITHOUT busy-spin:
- Lower `FLUSH_INTERVAL` from 10ms to ~4ms (the 64KB FLUSH_SIZE_BYTES still bounds IPC volume during bursts, so coalescing during tab-completion/file-dumps is preserved).
- Replace the fixed 10ms WouldBlock sleep (line ~351) with an ADAPTIVE nap: short (~2ms) when `!pending.is_empty()` (bytes are waiting for the next flush — keep latency low), longer (~8ms) when truly idle (`pending.is_empty()` — keep idle CPU low). e.g. `let nap = if pending.is_empty() { 8 } else { 2 }; std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(nap));`
- Do NOT change the UTF-8 leftover handling, the flush-before-sleep ordering, the size-threshold flush, or the stop-signal/EOF logic — only the two timing constants/expression.

This drops the keystroke-echo floor from ~10-20ms to ~4-6ms while keeping an idle reader at ~125 wakeups/sec (8ms) rather than spinning. Final gate: `pnpm build` clean, `pnpm -C apps/desktop test` clean, `cargo test --lib` clean (use --lib to dodge the running-app exe lock). SC5 (typing/output feels snappier) is interactive — user-verified in tauri:dev.
verification: FLUSH_INTERVAL reduced (~4ms); WouldBlock sleep is adaptive (short when pending, longer when idle)
UTF-8 leftover / flush-before-sleep / size-threshold / EOF / stop-signal logic unchanged
cargo test --lib clean; pnpm build clean; pnpm -C apps/desktop test clean
(visual/interactive, user) typing + heavy output feel snappier in tauri:dev; idle CPU not noticeably higher
phases.summary: pty.rs reader: FLUSH_INTERVAL 10ms->4ms; WouldBlock sleep replaced with adaptive nap (2ms while pending, 8ms idle). UTF-8/flush-before-sleep/size-threshold/EOF/stop logic untouched. Echo floor ~10-20ms -> ~4-6ms; idle ~125 wakeups/sec (no spin). Gate: cargo test --lib 52, pnpm build clean, desktop vitest 208. Also fixed a try-scoped id ref in phase-1 catch (caught by gate).
